title: OFAC SDN List: Sanctions match behavior
content: A match against the OFAC Specially Designated Nationals list indicates a direct sanctions obligation. Transacting with SDN-listed entities is prohibited under US law regardless of transaction size or apparent business purpose. Immediate escalation and legal review are required.
remediation: Block the transaction immediately, file SAR within required window, notify compliance officer, and consult legal counsel.

title: FATF Typology: Structuring (Smurfing)
content: Structuring involves breaking large cash amounts into smaller deposits to evade reporting thresholds. Multiple below-threshold transactions from same or related sources is a primary indicator. SAR filing is typically required when structuring is suspected.
remediation: File SAR if structuring is suspected, document evidence, and escalate to AML compliance team.

title: FATF Typology: Round-tripping and circular transactions
content: Round-tripping involves moving funds through a series of transactions that eventually return to the origin, creating the appearance of legitimate business activity. Circular payment patterns through shell entities are a confirmed typology.
remediation: Trace the complete funds cycle, identify beneficial ownership, and file SAR with full chain documentation.

title: FATF Typology: Shell company layering
content: Shell companies with no apparent business purpose are used to obscure beneficial ownership and move funds through multiple jurisdictions. Complex corporate structures that impede beneficial ownership identification are a primary layering indicator.
remediation: Identify ultimate beneficial owner, apply enhanced due diligence, document corporate structure, and file SAR if concealment is confirmed.

title: EU AMLD: Politically Exposed Person risk indicator
content: Transactions involving Politically Exposed Persons or their close associates require enhanced due diligence under EU AMLD Article 20. PEP status alone does not indicate wrongdoing but triggers enhanced scrutiny obligations.
remediation: Apply enhanced due diligence, obtain senior management approval, document source of funds and wealth, and monitor transactions continuously.

title: FATF Typology: Trade-based money laundering
content: Trade-based money laundering uses international trade transactions to transfer value. Over- and under-invoicing, multiple invoicing for the same shipment, and misrepresentation of goods are primary indicators.
remediation: Verify trade documentation against market prices, check for multiple invoicing, and apply enhanced scrutiny to high-risk trade corridors.

title: EU AMLD: Adverse media screening indicator
content: Negative news, adverse media, and watchlist hits are risk indicators that trigger enhanced due diligence obligations under EU AMLD. Financial crime, corruption, fraud, or sanctions-related adverse media materially increases customer risk rating.
remediation: Document adverse media findings, update risk rating, apply enhanced due diligence, and consider SAR where appropriate.

title: FinCEN: Unusual transaction velocity indicator
content: A sudden increase in transaction frequency, volume, or value inconsistent with a customer's historical profile or stated business is a primary SAR indicator. Velocity anomalies are especially significant when combined with high-risk jurisdictions or counterparties.
remediation: Compare against historical baseline, document anomalous pattern, request business justification, and file SAR if unexplained.

title: EU AMLD: High-risk third-country transaction indicator
content: Transactions involving third countries identified by the European Commission as having strategic AML/CFT deficiencies require enhanced due diligence under AMLD Article 18a. FATF grey and black-listed jurisdictions also trigger elevated scrutiny.
remediation: Apply enhanced due diligence, document transaction purpose and beneficial ownership, obtain senior management approval.

title: FATF Typology: Cryptocurrency mixing and chain-hopping
content: The use of cryptocurrency mixers, tumblers, or chain-hopping across multiple blockchains to obscure transaction history is a confirmed money laundering typology. Virtual asset service providers must screen for mixer interactions.
remediation: Screen for mixer addresses, trace cross-chain movements, apply enhanced due diligence, and file SAR if obfuscation is confirmed.

title: FinCEN: SAR filing obligation trigger
content: Financial institutions must file a Suspicious Activity Report within 30 calendar days of detecting a suspicious transaction of $5,000 or more. SAR non-filing is itself a regulatory violation. Multiple indicators combined increase SAR obligation likelihood.
remediation: Assess SAR filing obligation, document the decision, and file within the statutory 30-day window if threshold is met.

title: EU AMLD: Beneficial ownership opacity risk
content: Inability to identify the ultimate beneficial owner of a legal entity is a significant AML risk indicator. AMLD requires identification and verification of all UBOs holding 25% or more. Layered corporate structures designed to obscure UBOs require enhanced scrutiny.
remediation: Identify and verify all beneficial owners, document ownership structure, and apply enhanced CDD if UBO cannot be confirmed.

title: FATF Typology: Hawala and informal value transfer
content: Informal value transfer systems such as hawala operate outside formal financial channels, lack audit trails, and are a confirmed money laundering and terror financing typology. Use of hawala networks in high-value transactions is a primary AML risk indicator.
remediation: Document the informal transfer, assess SAR filing obligation, and apply enhanced scrutiny to counterparty.

title: EU AMLD: Real estate money laundering patterns
content: Real estate is a primary money laundering vehicle. Cash purchases, nominee ownership, over-valuation, and rapid resale are confirmed typology indicators. EU AMLD requires enhanced due diligence for high-value real estate transactions.
remediation: Verify source of funds, check for nominee ownership, apply enhanced CDD, and document valuation rationale.

title: FinCEN: High-risk account activity cluster
content: Accounts showing multiple combined indicators — high velocity, unusual counterparties, cash-intensive activity, high-risk geography — form a risk cluster that substantially raises SAR filing probability. Individual indicators are weak; combinations are strong.
remediation: Document all indicators, conduct holistic risk assessment, escalate to senior AML officer, and file SAR if combination warrants.

title: FATF: Correspondent banking risk indicators
content: Correspondent banking relationships with respondent banks in high-risk jurisdictions or with weak AML controls create de-risking obligations. FATF requires due diligence on respondent banks and monitoring of nested correspondent transactions.
remediation: Conduct respondent bank due diligence, assess AML controls, and monitor nested correspondent activity.

title: EU AMLD: New products and technology risk assessment
content: New payment methods, DeFi protocols, and technology-enabled financial products may carry inherently higher AML risks due to anonymity features. EU AMLD Article 19 requires enhanced CDD for new products with higher anonymity.
remediation: Conduct risk assessment for new products, apply enhanced CDD where anonymity is present, document risk mitigants.

title: FinCEN: Layering phase indicators
content: Rapid movement of funds through multiple accounts, jurisdictions, and institutions with no apparent business purpose is a primary layering indicator. Funds that pass through three or more accounts or jurisdictions within 24-48 hours are a strong SAR trigger.
remediation: Trace complete funds movement, identify all accounts and jurisdictions, document pattern, and file SAR.

title: False-positive pattern: normal business variation
content: Routine, explainable business transactions such as regular payroll, known recurring suppliers, documented seasonal variation, or transparent corporate treasury activity are common false positives in AML monitoring. A known business rationale substantially reduces risk classification.
remediation: Document business rationale and confirm with relationship manager before filing SAR on routine transactions.
